page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the n2-standard-32 in the middle tier for most workloads. It demonstrates strong uncached large-block memory write bandwidth, reaching 85.05 GB/sec, which falls into the top 25% of tested configurations. However, its read-only large-block memory bandwidth is more moderate at 166.42 GB/sec. In compute-heavy tasks, the instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 27,193.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9,858.0. For specialized tasks, it handles static web serving at 21.35 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 26.98 million operations per second. Large language model inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 10% at 4.16 tokens/sec due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise applications without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The inclusion of AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI) provides hardware-level acceleration for cryptography and integer operations. This makes the instance well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most categories, highlighted by a strong uncached large-block memory write bandwidth of 85.05 GB/sec and a PassMark CPU score of 27,193.8. However, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM inference (4.16 tokens/sec for a 70B model) due to the absence of dedicated accelerators. The instance supports AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instructions, making it a cost-efficient, balanced choice for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required without specialized hardware overhead.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and AVX-512 support for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of system RAM.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and AVX-512 support for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per vCPU) with no local storage
Strong uncached large-block memory write bandwidth of 85.05 GB/sec
Hardware-level acceleration via AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
